title: Inside Trump’s Deal With the I.R.S. to Drop His $10 Billion Lawsuit
summary: Discussions among a group of lawyers with allegiance to the president were closely held. Some senior White House officials were said to have felt blindsided as the agreement took shape.

title: Trump Squeezes Immigrants by Cutting Them Off From Jobs, Health Care and Housing
summary: The methodically planned strategy is intended to pressure noncitizens, including many with legal status, to leave the United States.

title: Is JD Vance the 2028 Front Runner? Trump Has Questions.
summary: President Trump appears to see the matter of his heir as unsettled, adding a layer of tension to his relationship with Vice President JD Vance.

title: Trump Urges Canceling Freedom 250 Concerts After Artists Drop Out
summary: The president had earlier said on social media that he should take the place of “these highly paid, Third Rate ‘Artists,’” saying he gets “larger audiences than Elvis.”

title: Platner’s Wife Flagged His Sexual Texts With Other Women as Maine Senate Race Began
summary: The wife of Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate in Maine, told the campaign he had sent sexual messages to other women.

title: Senator Dan Sullivan Has a New Challenger in Alaska: Dan Sullivan
summary: Two candidates with the same name will be on the ballot seeking the same seat in the state’s primary. “It’s going to be confusing,” one Republican said.

title: How NextEra Energy Wielded Political Power in Florida
summary: NextEra, which is seeking to buy Dominion Energy, has often butted heads with consumer groups, residents and journalists in its home state.

title: In the Los Angeles Mayoral Race, Restaurants Are One of the Hottest Issues
summary: In advance of Tuesday’s down-to-the-wire mayoral primary, several candidates have made the hospitality industry part of their platforms.

title: Inside the Ebola Epicenter, the Virus Rages With Little to Stop It
summary: A remote gold mining town is under siege, as medical workers struggle to beat back a surge of deaths and infections.

title: What to Know About the Ebola Outbreak
summary: Aid agencies are racing to help underequipped health workers in the Democratic Republic of Congo. More than 245 people are now suspected to have died from the virus.

title: Trump Sends Tougher Terms to Iran for Peace Framework, Officials Say
summary: His changes to the proposed deal were potentially designed to speed up the process by putting pressure Iran to accept the current framework, one official said.

title: Iran’s Team Trains in Limbo for World Cup Overshadowed by War
summary: The Iranian soccer players, still awaiting visas to the U.S., are practicing in Turkey and making backup plans.

title: Want to ‘Optimize’ Your Happiness? This Happiness Expert Says: Don’t.
summary: Laurie Santos on what will really bring meaning and fulfillment to your life, and what won’t.

title: An Afternoon in Brooklyn with Mayor Mamdani and a Bar Full of Arsenal Fans
summary: Zohran Mamdani watched his favorite team, Arsenal, compete for the Champions League title with fans at a bar, getting a rare taste of his old life after a meteoric rise in politics.

title: What the Meat Industry Doesn’t Want You to Know
summary: I once raised pigs. And factory farms raise them today in conditions that are as unconscionable as they are invisible.

title: He’s Entitled and Nepotistic. This Is Not What Democrats Need.
summary: Members of the country’s grandest political dynasty are not inherently qualified to hold office.

title: C.I.A. Officer Arrested With Gold Once Worked With No. 2 Pentagon Official
summary: David Rush, a longtime C.I.A. officer, appears to have first had contact with Stephen A. Feinberg during President Trump’s first term. Some officials said the two men were not close.

title: Meteor Over Boston Area Causes Loud Boom That Puzzled Residents
summary: A fireball reached 75,000 miles per hour before breaking up miles above New England, NASA said, causing a boom that alarmed residents. The incident followed a similar event in South Carolina.

title: How Curry Shops Got Caught in Japan’s Immigration Crackdown
summary: Under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, new visa rules are forcing some foreign business owners, who have put down roots in Japan, to leave.
